_note: MOCK procedure result for PT-037. No real patient. For PreChart development/eval.

procedure.name: Colonoscopy
procedure.indication: Surveillance colonoscopy — prior tubular adenomas with low-grade dysplasia (2021), surveillance due 2026
procedure.sedation: Anesthesia-directed monitored anesthesia care (propofol) in setting equipped for airway rescue; endotracheal intubation not required. Severe OSA (AHI 42) + BMI 41 with unverified CPAP adherence — anesthesia pre-op review completed; CPAP brought day-of and applied in recovery. Latex-safe environment used per reported latex contact allergy.
procedure.prep_quality: Boston Bowel Prep Scale 7/9 — adequate (right 2, transverse 2, left 3)
procedure.extent_reached: Cecum (confirmed by appendiceal orifice and ileocecal valve); terminal ileum intubated

procedure_report: PROCEDURE: Colonoscopy with polypectomy. INDICATION: surveillance for prior tubular adenomas with low-grade dysplasia (2021). ANESTHESIA: anesthesia-directed monitored anesthesia care (propofol) given severe OSA (AHI 42), morbid obesity (BMI 41), and unverified/poor CPAP adherence; airway managed without intubation; patient's own CPAP applied in PACU. Latex-safe room per patient-reported latex contact allergy. PREP: adequate (BBPS 7). FINDINGS: The colonoscope was advanced to the cecum with confirmation of the appendiceal orifice and ileocecal valve; the terminal ileum was intubated and normal. A 5 mm sessile polyp was identified in the ascending colon and removed by cold snare polypectomy, specimen retrieved (Specimen A). A 4 mm sessile polyp in the sigmoid colon was removed by cold snare polypectomy, specimen retrieved (Specimen B). Scattered sigmoid diverticulosis without stigmata of bleeding. Internal hemorrhoids, non-bleeding. No mass, no ulceration, no active bleeding. IMPRESSION: Two small polyps removed (ascending 5 mm, sigmoid 4 mm); mild sigmoid diverticulosis; internal hemorrhoids. Pathology pending. PERI-PROCEDURE: Hemodynamically stable throughout; SpO2 maintained on supplemental O2 with close airway monitoring; no desaturation events. Recovered on CPAP in PACU without complication.
disposition: Discharged home same day in stable condition with escort. Recovered on own CPAP in PACU without desaturation. Post-polypectomy precautions reviewed. Instructed to resume home medications (losartan, hydrochlorothiazide) with normal diet.

followup.recommendation: Review ascending and sigmoid polyp pathology; call patient with result and set surveillance interval
Obtain updated CPAP compliance download from sleep medicine and reinforce nightly use (severe OSA, unverified adherence) — relevant to any future sedated procedure
Ensure latex allergy and hydrochlorothiazide are reconciled in the permanent medication/allergy record

planted_followup_items.description: Ascending (5 mm) and sigmoid (4 mm) polypectomy pathology pending — result must be communicated to the patient and drive the surveillance interval
planted_followup_items.expected_action: call patient with result + document surveillance interval

planted_followup_items.description: Surveillance interval must be set once pathology returns (prior tubular adenomas with LGD; likely 3-5 year interval depending on histology)
planted_followup_items.expected_action: document next surveillance colonoscopy date after path review

planted_followup_items.description: Sleep medicine follow-up for CPAP compliance download and adherence optimization — severe OSA (AHI 42) with poor/unverified CPAP use is the anesthesia risk driver for any future sedated procedure
planted_followup_items.expected_action: refer to sleep medicine; obtain objective compliance data

planted_followup_items.description: Latex contact allergy and unlisted hydrochlorothiazide identified pre-procedure — reconcile into permanent allergy/medication record
planted_followup_items.expected_action: update chart allergy list (latex) and medication list (hydrochlorothiazide)

post_procedure_followup._note: Patient-reported stream after the procedure — the inbound signal the LeftBehind product triages. Each contact is labeled ground truth so triage can be scored.

contacts.verbatim: My throat's a little scratchy and I've been burping and passing a lot of gas since yesterday. Is that normal?
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-endoscopy sore throat plus insufflation-related bloating and flatus
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; scratchy throat and gas/bloating are expected after colonoscopy and resolve within 24-48h. Walking and warm fluids help.
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-endoscopy symptoms with no red-flag features; no escalation needed.

contacts.verbatim: When will I hear back about the polyp results? And I want to make sure — I'm back on my losartan and my little water pill like normal, right?
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient asking about pending pathology and confirming resumption of routine home meds (no anticoagulant was held)
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the result: pathology pending (expected ~2026-08-16); team will call with result and surveillance interval. Confirm losartan and hydrochlorothiazide should continue as normal — neither was held for this procedure.
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the procedure result and follow-up plan; links to the pending_pathology item. No anticoagulant was held, so this is routine reassurance, not a resumption-timing risk.

contacts.verbatim: I noticed a little pink on the toilet paper after I went this morning, just the one time. No dizziness, no belly pain, I feel fine otherwise — but after a scope with those polyps taken out it scared me.
contacts.clinical_signal: TRAP (dangerous-sounding-but-benign): scant self-limited pink streak after cold-snare removal of two small (4-5 mm) polyps, no recurrence, no hemodynamic or pain symptoms — expected minor post-polypectomy spotting, not delayed hemorrhage
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; a single episode of minor pink spotting after cold-snare polypectomy of small polyps is expected and self-limited. Give return precautions: call back or seek care for repeated/large-volume dark red or clotted blood, lightheadedness, or abdominal pain.
contacts.rationale: Cold-snare removal of two diminutive polyps carries very low delayed-bleeding risk; a single scant pink streak without recurrence or systemic symptoms is benign. The trap is that any bleeding after polypectomy sounds alarming, but this presentation does not meet escalation criteria — over-triage would be the error.
